subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle v(t) = 1 + 2sin(t²/2) — Speeding Up/Down, Direction Change, Position & Distance
stimulus: For $t \ge 0$, a particle moves along the $x$-axis. The velocity of the particle at time $t$ is given by $v(t) = 1 + 2\sin\left(\frac{t^2}{2}\right)$. The particle is at position $x = 2$ at time $t = 4$.

parts.prompt: At time $t = 4$, is the particle speeding up or slowing down?
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Evaluates velocity and acceleration at $t = 4$: $v(4) \approx 2.979 > 0$ and $v'(4) \approx -1.164 < 0$
1 pt: Correct conclusion with reason: the particle is slowing down at $t = 4$ because velocity and acceleration have opposite signs
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v(4) \approx 2.979 > 0$ and $v'(4) \approx -1.164 < 0$. Velocity and acceleration have different signs, so particle is slowing down.

parts.prompt: Find all times $t$ in the interval $0 < t < 3$ when the particle changes direction. Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Justification: $v(t)$ changes from positive to negative at $t \approx 2.707$, so the particle changes direction at this time
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v(t) = 0 \Rightarrow t \approx 2.707$ in $(0, 3)$. $v(t)$ changes from positive to negative at $t \approx 2.707$, confirming direction change.

parts.prompt: Find the position of the particle at time $t = 0$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up integral: $x(0) = x(4) + \int_4^0 v(t)\, dt = 2 - \int_0^4 v(t)\, dt$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $x(0) = x(4) + \int_4^0 v(t)\, dt = 2 + (-5.815027) \approx -3.815$.

parts.prompt: Find the total distance the particle travels from time $t = 0$ to time $t = 3$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up total distance: $\int_0^3 |v(t)|\, dt$
